docs.title: bspwm Dotfiles
docs.text:
Welcome to my custom bspwm configuration! This repository contains a highly polished, efficient, and keyboard-driven setup for the bspwm tiling window manager.

bspwm (Binary Space Partitioning Window Manager) is a lightweight tiling window manager for X11 that represents windows as the leaves of a full binary tree. It uses external programs for keyboard shortcuts (sxhkd) and panel (like polybar or waybar), following the Unix philosophy of doing one thing and doing it well.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Binary Space Partitioning: Windows are automatically tiled in a binary tree layout
  • sxhkd Integration: Clean, external hotkey daemon for keyboard shortcuts
  • Dual Conky Setup: Beautiful system monitoring with sqi-conky-theme on both sides
  • Pre-configured Keybinds: Comprehensive keyboard shortcuts for efficient workflow
  • Custom Wallpapers: Curated selection of beautiful backgrounds
  • Lightweight & Fast: Minimal resource usage for maximum performance

docs.title: Conky Setup
docs.text: This configuration features a beautiful dual conky setup using the sqi-conky-theme on both the left and right sides of your screen.

docs.title: sqi-conky-theme
docs.text:
The configuration uses two conky instances: - Left panel: sqi-left.conkyrc - Right panel: sqi-right.conkyrc

To manually start conky: conky -c ~/.config/conky/sqi-left.conkyrc & conky -c ~/.config/conky/sqi-right.conkyrc &

docs.title: Gallery
docs.text: Here are some screenshots of the bspwm configuration in action!

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Follow these steps to install and set up this bspwm configuration on your Arch Linux system.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Install the required packages from the official repositories:

```bash sudo pacman -S bspwm sxhkd xorg-xinit conky rofi thunar qterminal firefox-nightly maim brightnessctl dunst

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Clone or download this repository.

  2. Copy the configuration files to your ~/.config/ directory:

```bash cp -r configs/bspwm/ ~/.config/bspwm/ cp -r configs/sxhkd/ ~/.config/sxhkd/ cp -r config/sxhkd/rofi/ ~/.config/sxhkd/rofi cp -r configs/conky/ ~/.config/conky/

chmod +x ~/.config/bspwm/bspwmrc

docs.title: Keybinds
docs.text: bspwm uses sxhkd (Simple X Hotkey Daemon) for keyboard shortcuts. All keybinds are configured in ~/.config/sxhkd/sxhkdrc.

docs.title: Volume Control
docs.text: Shortcut Action XF86AudioRaiseVolume Increase volume by 3% XF86AudioLowerVolume Decrease volume by 3% XF86AudioMute Toggle mute

docs.title: Brightness Control
docs.text: Shortcut Action XF86MonBrightnessUp Increase brightness by 5% XF86MonBrightnessDown Decrease brightness by 5%

docs.title: Screenshots
docs.text: Shortcut Action Super + Print Full screenshot Super + Shift + Print Select area screenshot

docs.title: Applications
docs.text: Shortcut Action Super + Return Open terminal (qterminal) Super + d Open application launcher (rofi) Super + e Open file manager (thunar) Super + w Open web browser (firefox-nightly)

docs.title: System
docs.text: Shortcut Action Super + Escape Reload sxhkd configuration Super + Alt + q Quit bspwm Super + Alt + r Restart bspwm

docs.title: Window Management
docs.text: Shortcut Action Super + q Close window Super + Shift + q Kill window Super + m Toggle monocle layout Super + t Set tiled state Super + Shift + t Set pseudo-tiled state Super + s Set floating state Super + f Set fullscreen state

docs.title: Focus & Navigation
docs.text: Shortcut Action Super + h/j/k/l Focus window in direction Super + Shift + h/j/k/l Swap window in direction Super + [1-9,0] Focus desktop 1-10 Super + Shift + [1-9,0] Send window to desktop 1-10 Super + Left/Right Focus previous/next desktop Super + Grave Focus last node/desktop Super + Tab Focus last node/desktop

docs.title: Preselection
docs.text: Shortcut Action Super + Ctrl + h/j/k/l Preselect direction Super + Ctrl + [1-9] Preselect ratio Super + Ctrl + Space Cancel preselection

docs.title: Move & Resize
docs.text: Shortcut Action Super + Alt + h/j/k/l Expand window side Super + Alt + Shift + h/j/k/l Contract window side Super + Arrow Keys Move floating window

docs.title: Editing Keybinds
docs.text: To modify keybinds, edit ~/.config/sxhkd/sxhkdrc and reload with Super + Escape.

docs.title: Theming
docs.text: bspwm itself is minimal and doesn't have themes in the traditional sense. However, this configuration includes several theming elements:

docs.title: Configuration Files
docs.text:
• bspwmrc: Main configuration script that sets up bspwm rules, borders, and gaps
  • sxhkdrc: Keyboard shortcuts configuration

docs.title: Window Borders
docs.text: bspwm window borders are configured in the bspwmrc file. You can customize: - Border width - Border colors (focused, normal, urgent, etc.) - Window gaps

docs.title: Wallpapers
docs.text:
This setup includes a Wallpapers directory with beautiful backgrounds including: - Black and white photography - Pink Floyd Dark Side of the Moon prism - San Francisco cityscape

To set a wallpaper manually:

```bash feh --bg-fill ~/.config/bspwm/Wallpapers/BlackSanFrancisco.jpg
